key_issues: No blocking issues. All acceptance criteria pass and subtle correctness properties were runtime-verified by the reviewer (falsy-data carriage, reference identity, absent-vs-null omission, -32603 fallback x2, contract rejection of non-int code / non-string message).

NOTE (non-blocking): exn->jsonrpc-error-jsexpr return contract is hash?, slightly looser than the hash-eq?/immutable? value actually produced; the test asserts hash-eq? independently so it is harmless.
NOTE (non-blocking): encoder maps any non-mcp exn (incl. exn:break if ever handed one) to -32603; implementer correctly documented that S3 lets breaks propagate so this is never reached.

Additive export deviation (jsexpr-value? added to spec-2025-11-25.rkt provide) is ACCEPTABLE and is the correct DRY choice: purely additive, no behavior change, spec module + types facade still load, reuses the exact predicate behind jsonrpc-error/c.
